title: When a new column is created, it shouldn't be added to every View
excerpt: Having to adjust each view after column creation is clunky and painful. It seems unintended, and busy managers end up leaving a mess because they don’t have time to go back in and clean up many views on many boards each …

posts.author: CHOCM (Christopher Herndon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Having to adjust each view after column creation is clunky and painful. It seems unintended, and busy managers end up leaving a mess because they don’t have time to go back in and clean up many views on many boards each time a new column is needed.

Here are some other versions of this same request!

Aug 2022

New Columns and Views Feature requests
Is it possible to NOT add a new column to every view in a board automatically? Very annoying to have to manually toggle visibility in every view every time a new column is added and intended for just one view.

Mar 2023

For Views that have hidden columns, default it so newly added columns are automatically hidden for those Views Feature requests
Views are great in Monday — mostly. One problem I’m having and don’t see a solution for is that adding new columns shows up on all of the views. I have boards with 10 or more view tabs and when I add a column to one (or to the Main Table), that new column gets appended to all of the other views as being visible. Not all views necessarily need to see that new column, so it becomes a pain to keep cleaning up my existing Views. This has really reared its ugly head with regards to using the Baselin…

Feb 2024

Add a column to a specific view without having to hide it on the rest of the views. Feature requests
Currently if you add a new column to any view of the board - it will appear on all the views of the same board. So you need to go and hide this new column from the other views. It would be nice to have options: if you need to add a column to a specific view - column will be added only to this view (and main table of course), but not to the rest of the views. if you want a column to appear on all views - you just add it on the main table. Currently when people start working with the views - t…

posts.author: MaxArceneaux (Max)
posts.cookedHtml:
I 100% agree, it’s absolutely insane that we have to deal with this at scale…

I actually had a fix pushed to my account about a year ago, and the result was that ALL of the columns in the affected board were completely reordered… Completely ruined the trust that I had spent building up with a stakeholder. Looks like the patch has since been removed from my Org.

I cannot beleive we have to deal with this in 2025 while the development team continues to push out boilerplate AI slop…

posts.author: Alex_APP (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: Even if there is a toggle that allows you to choose which views you want it to be available on before posting. It just takes so much time to go through and remove from all the views that the column showed up in. When you have dozens of views and hundreds of columns to work with, this takes up more time than it is all even worth

posts.author: YSteinberg (Yoel S)
posts.cookedHtml: This is highly irritating to us as well. Voted.

posts.author: mightywink (Alicia Reed)
posts.cookedHtml: I discovered that if you add a column from a custom View (and not from the Main Table) that new column will only show on that specific View by default. So that is one small work-around if you need it to only show on one View. Otherwise, I agree 100% that there needs to be a more dynamic “view selection” process when adding a column.
